solution: mosmb server machine should have enough memory space left for mosmb service to use
Freeing up the cache memory may help
(sync; echo 3 > /prco/sys/vm/drop_cache) Make sure that server machine has enough swap space available
Please go through the system requirements here - System Requirements

error_string: Configuration Server init failed OR Configuration file empty
solution: Please check mosmb configuration at - /etc/mosmb/mosmb.json Make sure that suitable values based on /etc/mosmb/README.md OR refer to configuration parameter details Verify the parameter values using mosmbcli for the validation
mosmbcli - validate

error_string: Failed getpwnam. username = %s, errno is : %d & Id mapper service not configured correctly
solution: For all Active Directory users 1
Ensure /etc/nsswitch.conf is configured correctly nsswitch.conf Configuration 2
Check sssd configurations w.r.t authentication mode required
krb5 - SSSD Configuration ntlmAD - SSSD Configuration For local linux users please check if nsswitch.conf passwd has files as first option.

error_string: Failed getpwnam. username = %s, errno is : %d & Id mapper service not configured correctly
solution: For all Active Directory users 1
Ensure /etc/nsswitch.conf is configured correctly nsswitch.conf Configuration 2
Check sssd configurations w.r.t authentication mode required
krb5 - SSSD Configuration ntlmAD - SSSD Configuration For local linux users please check if nsswitch.conf passwd has files as first option.
check /etc/resolv.conf settings make sure correct domain name and its IP are mentioned

Add linux local user as mosmb users for ntlmLocal access using mosmb_passwd binary with -a option as mosmb_passwd -a <user> This wil generate/update the file named /etc/mosmb/mosmb_u.dat mosmb_passwd uitility
Add machine password using mosmb_passwd binary with -m option as mosmb_passwd -m Make sure adcli package is present on the server machine for this binary to work
This will generate/update the file named /etc/mosmb/domain_password & /etc/krb5.keytab mosmb_passwd uitility

error_string: Internal domain list is empty. Please check if entries in domain_list.txt are valid
solution: This error needs further investigation the cause for internal domain list to be empty
MoSMB logs during Initialization (service start) should mention why
Possible causes are: Failed to connect/verify with ldap Failed to connect to RPC server Make sure that all required ports are open and LDAP and RCP servers are reachable
mosmb ports to enable on firewall If all connectivity is as expected please contact us at support@ryussi.com and share the mosmb logs and json files
Configuration Verification & Collection

error_string: Internal domain list is empty. Please check if entries in domain_list.txt are valid
solution: This error needs further investigation the cause for internal domain list to be empty
MoSMB logs during Initialization (service start) should mention why
Possible causes are: Failed to connect/verify with ldap Failed to connect to RPC server Make sure that all required ports are open and LDAP and RCP servers are reachable
mosmb ports to enable on firewall If all connectivity is as expected please contact us at support@ryussi.com and share the mosmb logs and json files
Configuration Verification & Collection
Check kerberos settings
Check time is in sync with the AD Check the keytab creation is correctly done using ktpass on AD by the Admin (w.o
any errors or warning during the creation) using correct CRYPTO values used to generate keys in the keytab

Use following PS cmdlet to check the spn is correctly mapped the special user setspn -Q cifs/<cifs-server-name> And use the follwoing PS cmdlet to check the user's SPN Get-ADUser <username> -property ServicePrincipalName The DNS forward lookup works for the cluster fqdn and reverse lookup work correctly for cluster node ips Check firewall for correct ports opened for kerberos Check that the special user created for keytab creation in the ktpass 'mapuser', has following properties set: Go to Active Directory users and computers User properties -> user account -> password never expires User properties -> user account -> Correct encryption support (in accordance with the CRYPTO values used in ktpass) is enabled This account supports Kerberos AES 256 bit encryption This account supports Kerberos AES 128 bit encryption OR use following PS cmdlet to check the user's supported encryption types Get-ADUser <username> -property KerberosEncryptionType Check KVno from keytab against the special user

Note the msDS-KeyVersionNumber from the o/p of following PS cmdlet and compare with kvno in the keytab from the output of klist ran on the mosmb.keytab Get-ADUser <username> -property msDS-KeyVersionNumber klist -kt /etc/mosmb/mosmb.keytab In mosmb.json, check that globalSection is set to have enableHighAvailability to yes Check sssd.conf, krb5.conf, ldap.conf are set in accordance with the mosmb documentation Check permitted encryption types from the krb5 crypto-policies cat /etc/krb5.conf.d/crypto-policies
Check kerberos settings
Check time is in sync with the AD Check the keytab creation is correctly done using ktpass on AD by the Admin (w.o
any errors or warning during the creation) using correct CRYPTO values used to generate keys in the keytab

Use following PS cmdlet to check the spn is correctly mapped the special user setspn -Q cifs/<cifs-server-name> And use the follwoing PS cmdlet to check the user's SPN Get-ADUser <username> -property ServicePrincipalName The DNS forward lookup works for the cluster fqdn and reverse lookup work correctly for cluster node ips Check firewall for correct ports opened for kerberos Check that the special user created for keytab creation in the ktpass 'mapuser', has following properties set: Go to Active Directory users and computers User properties -> user account -> password never expires User properties -> user account -> Correct encryption support (in accordance with the CRYPTO values used in ktpass) is enabled This account supports Kerberos AES 256 bit encryption This account supports Kerberos AES 128 bit encryption OR use following PS cmdlet to check the user's supported encryption types Get-ADUser <username> -property KerberosEncryptionType Check KVno from keytab against the special user

Note the msDS-KeyVersionNumber from the o/p of following PS cmdlet and compare with kvno in the keytab from the output of klist ran on the mosmb.keytab Get-ADUser <username> -property msDS-KeyVersionNumber klist -kt /etc/mosmb/mosmb.keytab In mosmb.json, check that globalSection is set to have enableHighAvailability to yes Check sssd.conf, krb5.conf, ldap.conf are set in accordance with the mosmb documentation Check permitted encryption types from the krb5 crypto-policies cat /etc/krb5.conf.d/crypto-policies
Check kerberos settings
Check time is in sync with the AD Check the keytab creation is correctly done using ktpass on AD by the Admin (w.o
any errors or warning during the creation) using correct CRYPTO values used to generate keys in the keytab

Use following PS cmdlet to check the spn is correctly mapped the special user setspn -Q cifs/<cifs-server-name> And use the follwoing PS cmdlet to check the user's SPN Get-ADUser <username> -property ServicePrincipalName The DNS forward lookup works for the cluster fqdn and reverse lookup work correctly for cluster node ips Check firewall for correct ports opened for kerberos Check that the special user created for keytab creation in the ktpass 'mapuser', has following properties set: Go to Active Directory users and computers User properties -> user account -> password never expires User properties -> user account -> Correct encryption support (in accordance with the CRYPTO values used in ktpass) is enabled This account supports Kerberos AES 256 bit encryption This account supports Kerberos AES 128 bit encryption OR use following PS cmdlet to check the user's supported encryption types Get-ADUser <username> -property KerberosEncryptionType Check KVno from keytab against the special user

Note the msDS-KeyVersionNumber from the o/p of following PS cmdlet and compare with kvno in the keytab from the output of klist ran on the mosmb.keytab Get-ADUser <username> -property msDS-KeyVersionNumber klist -kt /etc/mosmb/mosmb.keytab In mosmb.json, check that globalSection is set to have enableHighAvailability to yes Check sssd.conf, krb5.conf, ldap.conf are set in accordance with the mosmb documentation Check permitted encryption types from the krb5 crypto-policies cat /etc/krb5.conf.d/crypto-policies

error_string: Limit of max Opens per file/folder reached for %s OC: %d
solution: Increase value of parameter "maxOpens" under "persistentStoreSection" in /etc/mosmb/mosmb.json

error_string: Limit of 250 Opens per file/folder reached for %s OC: %d
solution: Temporarily file is not accessible as more than 250 open on the file
Will be accessible once few file handles are closed

error_string: Failed to initialise session key with AD
solution: Cause of failure: 1
Check entries in the Domain list if correct
Check LDAP connectivity 3
Check DNS/nsswitch configuration 4
Check connectivity with RPC server 5

Check sssd.conf if it is modified to use ad_maximum_machine_account_password_age = 0 (If sssd renews the machine password then mosmb doesn't know the m/c password and that can cause ntlm-AD access failures.) If it is missing, add it and restart sssd , run mosmb_passwd -m and then restart mosmb

solution: This is an internal error message.
This is an internal error message
User has to do nothing.
This is an internal error but user can check if the pst file of particular node in cluster exist or not and also check if the correct permissions are given to the file.

error_string: Unable to take pst entries lock\n
solution: This is an internal error message
User has to do nothing.

error_string: Unable to release pst entries lock\n
solution: This is an internal error message
User has to do nothing.

error_string: Node id %d is out of range
solution: User or System admin should make sure that node id should be in range 1 to 1023.

error_string: mbr bitmap vinode lock init failed for %s
solution: This is an internal error message but user should check the system resources of the node.

error_string: mbr bitmap vinode lock failed with error %d
solution: This is an internal error message but user should check the system resources of the node.

error_string: mbr bitmap zoo get for %s failed with error %d
solution: This is an internal error message but user should check the system resources of the node.

error_string: FILE_FLAG_DELETE_ON_CLOSE could not be carried out on the file %s due to server failure.\n[ACTION]: System admin should manually delete this file.
solution: User or System admin should manually delete the mentioned file.

error_string: Unable to cleanup to remote open table
solution: User or System admin should make sure that distributed file system (DFS) is mounted and functioning properly.
User or System admin should make sure that distributed file system (DFS) is mounted and functioning properly.

error_string: Failed to unmap Memory Map for %s Errno %d
solution: User should make sure that distributed file system (DFS) is mounted and functioning properly.

error_string: The node id %d entry is not found in %s
solution: This is an internal error message and MoSMB server handles it internally
But user should make sure that MoSMB-zookeeper is functioning properly and also check that connectivity between MoSMB and MoSMB-zookeeper is functioning.

error_string: Failed to remove the %d entry from %s
solution: This is an internal error message and MoSMB server handles it internally
But user should make sure that MoSMB-zookeeper is functioning properly and also check that connectivity between MoSMB and MoSMB-zookeeper is functioning.
This is an internal error message but user should check the system resources of the node.
This is an internal error message but user should check the system resources of the node.

error_string: could not open cluster command interface
Failed write on cluster command interface

solution: Make sure that High Availability (HA) is enabled or not in /etc/mosmb/mosmb.json
Currently maximum node supported in single cluster by MoSMB server, is maximum upto 1023
So user should make sure that maximum nodes are 1023.
